pi_items.verified_value: Sygic collects general analysis of applications, analysis of user trends including general user profiling, and data for improvement & testing, with retention linked to active app usage
pi_items.source: Sygic official privacy policy at https://www.sygic.com/company/privacy-policy and Common Sense Privacy evaluation

pi_items.verified_value: Sygic privacy policy states 'users can revoke consent at any time' for data processing based on consent

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real Sygic GPS Navigation app (com.sygic.aura) running in an Android emulator. Sygic's official privacy policy confirms the company collects analytics, product improvement data, and user trend data linked to active app usage. Users can revoke consent for data processing at any time. The expected state for disabling analytics collection is OFF (disabled). However, the specific UI element keywords in the metadata ('Product improvement', 'Help improve Sygic', 'Usage statistics', 'Consent') could not be verified against any public documentation, privacy evaluations, user guides, or app stores. The Common Sense Privacy evaluation explicitly notes that detailed settings information was 'unanswered', suggesting these exact setting names may not be documented in public sources or may vary across app versions.
